title: Read access is allowed from inside read-action only
author: cmburn
body: **Describe the bug** Attempting to launch the debugger results in the following error message: ``` Read access is allowed from inside read-action only (see Application.​runReadAction()); If you access or modify model on EDT consider wrapping your code in WriteIntentReadAction or ReadAction; see https://​jb.​gg/​ij-platform-threading for details Current thread: Thread[#679723,DefaultDispatcher-worker-25,5,main] 1045710444 (EventQueue.​isDispatchThread()=false) SystemEventQueueThread: Thread[#44,AWT-EventQueue-0,6,InnocuousThreadGroup] 178429542 ``` This happens on both elixir 1.20.0 and 1.20.1. The error occurred both on an existing project and newly created project, both via `mix phx.new`. Any help would be welcome! **To Reproduce** Steps to reproduce the behavior: 1. Create new project from a terminal 2. Create any mix target (i.e. phx.server) 3. Launch the debugger **Expected behavior** Debugger should run until the first breakpoint and halt. **Screenshots** **Desktop:** - OS: Debian Linux - Version Testing **Erlang:** - Installer asdf - Version 16.0.1, OTP 28 **Elixir:** - Installer asdf - Version 1.20.0, OTP 28 **Plugin:** - Version [v23.9.0-pre-2](https://github.com/KronicDeth/intellij-elixir/releases/tag/v23.9.0-pre-2) **Additional context** Add any other context about the problem here.

title: Usage of internal APIs due to ProjectSdksModel to specify target EelMachine/EelDescriptor being internal
author: joshuataylor
body: Thanks @sh41 - forgot I replied back to them, and dug into the issue! (https://github.com/KronicDeth/intellij-elixir/pull/3814/changes#diff-22cb4b45f460c3897e1039977fff8e2c8e82c97195b52d200d316000fc3c0318R42) # Step 1: Submitted the plugin, got told we were using an Internal API. SO, when I submitted the last plugin, they denied it, due to using Internal APIs. Fair enough.. [```] Hello, Thank you for submitting your plugin. The latest update of your plugin uses an Internal API, which is private and cannot be used outside the IntelliJ Platform itself. Please proceed with the [next steps described in this article](https://plugins.jetbrains.com/docs/intellij/api-internal.html) to resolve the issue. ``` # Step 2: Email back to JetBrains, after spending a *delightful* evening again diving into the depths of the IDE internals I never want to see 🫢 .. --- Hi, Thank you for the review feedback. We are requesting an exception for the internal APi violations, which all come from a single class: `NonDefaultProjectWrapper` in [ElixirSdkForModuleStep.kt](https://github.com/KronicDeth/intellij-elixir/blob/main/src/org/elixir_lang/mix/project/_import/step/ElixirSdkForModuleStep.kt). ## What the code does Our plugin has an SDK selection step during Mix project import. [`ProjectSdksModel.reset(Project)`](https://github.com/JetBrains/intellij-community/blob/master/platform/lang-impl/src/com/intellij/openapi/roots/ui/configuration/projectRoot/ProjectSdksModel.java#L163-L197) uses the project's EEL environment to filter SDKs (e.g., showing only WSL SDKs when importing a WSL project). However, during import, the wizard context often provides the default project, which has a null `projectFilePath`. This causes [`Project.getEelDescriptor()`](https://github.com/JetBrains/intellij-community/blob/master/platform/eel-provider/src/com/intellij/platform/eel/provider/EelProvider.kt#L164-L177) to return `LocalEelDescriptor`, filtering out WSL/remote SDKs. To fix this, we wrap the default `Project` using Kotlin's `by delegate` pattern to override only two methods: - `isDefault()` returns `false` (so `reset()` takes the EEL resolution path) - `getProjectFilePath()` returns the actual import directory path (so EEL can detect WSL/remote) The wrapper is only ever passed to `ProjectSdksModel.reset()`, which calls `isDefault()`, `getProjectFilePath()`, and `getService()` on it. ## Why there seems to be no public API alternative - [`ProjectSdksModel.syncSdks(EelMachine)`](https://github.com/JetBrains/intellij-community/blob/master/platform/lang-impl/src/com/intellij/openapi/roots/ui/configuration/projectRoot/ProjectSdksModel.java#L114-L135) would be the clean alternative, but it is `@ApiStatus.Internal`. - [`ProjectSdksModel.sdkMatchesEel(EelMachine, Sdk)`](https://github.com/JetBrains/intellij-community/blob/master/platform/lang-impl/src/com/intellij/openapi/roots/ui/configuration/projectRoot/ProjectSdksModel.java#L137-L161) is also `@Internal`, so post-filtering is not possible either. - The public [`syncSdks()`](https://github.com/JetBrains/intellij-community/blob/master/platform/lang-impl/src/com/intellij/openapi/roots/ui/configuration/projectRoot/ProjectSdksModel.java#L107-L109) (no args) hardcodes `LocalEelMachine`. - The public `reset(Project)` is the only entry point, and it requires a non-default `Project` with a valid `projectFilePath` to enable EEL resolution. - JetBrains' own [`ProjectJdkForModuleStep`](https://github.com/JetBrains/intellij-community/blob/master/java/idea-ui/src/com/intellij/ide/util/projectWizard/ProjectJdkForModuleStep.java#L58-L62) has the same limitation -- it passes the default project directly, so EEL-aware SDK filtering does not work in import wizards. ## Why the violations might not be actual usage of internal APIs All 14 violations seem to be Kotlin delegation - `class Foo(private val delegate: Project) : Project by delegate` are Kotlin generated override methods for every method in the interface hierarchy, including all `@Internal` `ComponentManager` methods: - `createError(String, PluginId)` / `createError(Throwable, PluginId)` - `getActivityCategory(boolean)` (return type `ActivityCategory` is also `@Internal`) - `instantiateClass(Class, PluginId)` / `instantiateClass(String, PluginDescriptor)` - `instantiateClassWithConstructorInjection(Class, Object, PluginId)` - `isInjectionForExtensionSupported()` - `loadClass(String, PluginDescriptor)` Our code never calls any of these methods? The generated overrides simply delegate to the wrapped project instance. The only methods our code actually overrides are `isDefault()` and `getProjectFilePath()`, both of which are public API. ## What we are asking for We request an exception for these 14 violations on the grounds that: 1. There is no public API to achieve EEL-aware SDK filtering during project import. 2. The internal methods are never invoked by our code -- they are Kotlin delegation artifacts. 3. The wrapper is narrowly scoped (private inner class, used in one place). 4. We will file a YouTrack issue requesting a public API for this use case. Best regards, Josh --- ## Step 3: Approval, no other message... ## Step 4: Life got busy again, I never ended up making the YouTrack ## Step 5; @sh41 reaches out to them - https://youtrack.jetbrains.com/issue/IJPL-243914 ## Step 6: We get denied every time now until we email them back.. fun.

title: Can't add elixir SDK
author: irakliasatiani
body: **Describe the bug** In the Jetbrains version 2025.3.5, specifically picked 2025.3+, it detects erlang and elixir SDK, properly installed through ASDF, it selects Erlang SDK but when I click on elixir SDK, it just does nothing, the warning 'Project SDK is not defined' stays as it is. Plugin version is 23, so latest, I tried downgrading it to 22, still the same. but in that case elixir SDK is not visible in the 'settings' languages tab itself. plugins looks completely broken to me because of that. **Desktop:** - OS: MacOS (Silicon) - Tahoe 28.4.1 **Erlang:** - ASDF - Version 29, tried 28 as well, no luck **Elixir:** - ASDF - Latest (1.19) as well as (1.18.4) **Plugin:** - 23 and 22

title: Dev Container Erlang/Elixir SDKs are detected but cannot be added/assigned in IntelliJ-Elixir for EMQX
author: JiaRG
body: **Describe the bug** When using IntelliJ IDEA with a Dev Container for the EMQX repository, the IntelliJ-Elixir plugin can detect Erlang/Elixir SDKs inside the container after refreshing SDKs, but the detected SDKs cannot be added or assigned as Project SDKs / Module SDKs. **To Reproduce** Steps to reproduce the behavior: 1. Open the EMQX repository in IntelliJ IDEA 2. Use a Dev Container with the following configuration: ```json { "name": "emqx-dev", "image": "ghcr.io/emqx/emqx-builder/6.1-2:1.19.1-28.4.1-2-el7", "workspaceFolder": "/workspaces/emqx", "customizations": { "jetbrains": { "backend": "IntelliJ" } } } ``` 3. Connect IntelliJ IDEA to the Dev Container 4. Open the Elixir SDK menu 5. Click “Refresh All Elixir SDKs” IDEA reports: “Successfully refreshed 2 Elixir SDKs and 2 Erlang SDKs” 6. Open Project Structure → SDKs 7. Try to add/select Erlang or Elixir SDKs **Expected behavior** The Erlang/Elixir SDKs detected inside the Dev Container should be selectable and assignable as Project SDKs / Module SDKs. The plugin should properly support SDKs that are already installed inside the Dev Container image. **Screenshots** **Logs** No additional logs currently. **Desktop:** window10 **Erlang:** Installer: preinstalled inside Dev Container image Version: OTP 28.4.1 **Elixir:** Installer: preinstalled inside Dev Container image Version: 1.19.1 **Plugin:** - 23.0.5 **Additional context** The issue is reproducible with the EMQX repository and affects Mix/Rebar/Erlang tooling because SDKs cannot be properly assigned even though the plugin can detect them during refresh. The SDK refresh appears to partially work, but the detected SDKs do not become available in the SDK configuration UI.

title: Cannot import umbrella project with root and app having the same name ("Module already exists: emqx")
author: JiaRG
body:
**Describe the bug**
When importing an Elixir umbrella project in IntelliJ IDEA (with Elixir plugin), if the root project folder
and one of the child apps have the same name, the import fails with:

Module already exists: <name>
com.intellij.openapi.module.ModuleWithNameAlreadyExists

Example:
  Root project folder: emqx
  Child app: apps/emqx

The IDE requires unique module names, but the Elixir umbrella pattern often has a root project and an
app sharing the same name. Currently, there seems to be no way to override the module name during import
without renaming files/folders, which is undesirable.

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:

1. Clone EMQX umbrella project: https://github.com/emqx/emqx
2. Open IntelliJ IDEA → File → Open Project → Select root emqx directory `mix.exs` file
3. Import as Elixir Mix project
4. Observe import failure with Module already exists: emqx

**Expected behavior**
The IDE should allow specifying a module name for child apps when the name conflicts with the root project,
or automatically rename the IDEA module to avoid conflicts.

**Screenshots**
If applicable, add screenshots to help explain your problem.

**Logs**

title: function :int.interpretable/1 is undefined (module :int is not available) on OTP 27+
author: xeagle2
body:
**Describe the bug**
Running Mix ExUnit in Intellij fails with the following errors:

```
[error] GenServer IntelliJElixir.Debugger.Server terminating
** (UndefinedFunctionError) function :int.interpretable/1 is undefined (module :int is not available)
    :int.interpretable(JSON.Encoder)
    /private/var/folders/01/ctgb0ykn3mgd5zg6nmz_q7wh0000gn/T/intellij_elixir7/debugger/lib/intellij_elixir/debugger/server.ex:400: anonymous fn/1 in IntelliJElixir.Debugger.Server.interpret_modules_in/2
    (elixir 1.18.4) lib/stream.ex:536: anonymous fn/4 in Stream.filter/2
    (elixir 1.18.4) lib/enum.ex:4968: Enumerable.List.reduce/3
    (elixir 1.18.4) lib/stream.ex:1773: Enumerable.Stream.do_each/4
    (elixir 1.18.4) lib/enum.ex:4515: Enum.each/2
    /private/var/folders/01/ctgb0ykn3mgd5zg6nmz_q7wh0000gn/T/intellij_elixir7/debugger/lib/intellij_elixir/debugger/server.ex:401: IntelliJElixir.Debugger.Server.interpret_modules_in/2
    /private/var/folders/01/ctgb0ykn3mgd5zg6nmz_q7wh0000gn/T/intellij_elixir7/debugger/lib/intellij_elixir/debugger/server.ex:133: anonymous fn/3 in IntelliJElixir.Debugger.Server.handle_call/3

```

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Create a simple "hello-world" test
2. Trigger Run/Debug the test
3. See the error

**Expected behavior**
The Run/Debug operation should be launched successfully. 

**Desktop:**
 - OS: macOS 
 - Version 15.6.1

**Erlang:**
- Installer: asdf
- Version: 27.3.4.7

**Elixir:**
- Installer: asdf
- Version: 1.18.4-otp-27

**Plugin:**
- Version: 23.0.1

title: Potential read-action starvation / EDT freeze in 2025.3 (AI analysis, needs verification)
author: sh41
body:
## Summary (AI assistant analysis — please verify)
Frequent UI freezes in IntelliJ IDEA 2025.3.2 with intellij-elixir installed. Thread dump shows the EDT blocked waiting for a write lock while a read action appears long-running (writer preference in 2025.3). The EDT stack in the dump is parked in `SuvorovProgress.showNiceOverlay` during a VCS UI update (`CommitMessage.setText`).

This is an **AI assistant analysis and needs further investigation/verification**. The items below are speculative hotspots based on code inspection and the freeze context.

## Suspect areas (permalinks)
### Long-running work under read actions (no cancellation checks)
- Bulk BEAM decompilation runs a full PSI traversal inside `runReadAction` with no `checkCanceled()`:
  - https://github.com/KronicDeth/intellij-elixir/blob/042b07cf3615a4471252460c09123f86f13008e2/src/org/elixir_lang/beam/BulkDecompilation.kt#L78
  - https://github.com/KronicDeth/intellij-elixir/blob/042b07cf3615a4471252460c09123f86f13008e2/src/org/elixir_lang/beam/BulkDecompilation.kt#L80
  - https://github.com/KronicDeth/intellij-elixir/blob/042b07cf3615a4471252460c09123f86f13008e2/src/org/elixir_lang/beam/BulkDecompilation.kt#L88
  - https://github.com/KronicDeth/intellij-elixir/blob/042b07cf3615a4471252460c09123f86f13008e2/src/org/elixir_lang/beam/BulkDecompilation.kt#L90

- `DefinitionsScopedSearch` runs as `QueryExecutorBase(readAction = true)` and loops through protocol implementations/definitions without `ProgressManager.checkCanceled()` or early-exit on `consumer.process`:
  - https://github.com/KronicDeth/intellij-elixir/blob/042b07cf3615a4471252460c09123f86f13008e2/src/org/elixir_lang/DefinitionsScopedSearch.kt#L20
  - https://github.com/KronicDeth/intellij-elixir/blob/042b07cf3615a4471252460c09123f86f13008e2/src/org/elixir_lang/DefinitionsScopedSearch.kt#L46
  - https://github.com/KronicDeth/intellij-elixir/blob/042b07cf3615a4471252460c09123f86f13008e2/src/org/elixir_lang/DefinitionsScopedSearch.kt#L54
  - https://github.com/KronicDeth/intellij-elixir/blob/042b07cf3615a4471252460c09123f86f13008e2/src/org/elixir_lang/DefinitionsScopedSearch.kt#L81
  - https://github.com/KronicDeth/intellij-elixir/blob/042b07cf3615a4471252460c09123f86f13008e2/src/org/elixir_lang/DefinitionsScopedSearch.kt#L91
  - https://github.com/KronicDeth/intellij-elixir/blob/042b07cf3615a4471252460c09123f86f13008e2/src/org/elixir_lang/DefinitionsScopedSearch.kt#L105

- Credo global inspection loops over `stdoutLines` and repeatedly calls blocking `runReadAction` without cancellation checks (lock churn in large outputs):
  - https://github.com/KronicDeth/intellij-elixir/blob/042b07cf3615a4471252460c09123f86f13008e2/src/org/elixir_lang/credo/inspection_tool/Global.kt#L124
  - https://github.com/KronicDeth/intellij-elixir/blob/042b07cf3615a4471252460c09123f86f13008e2/src/org/elixir_lang/credo/inspection_tool/Global.kt#L138
  - https://github.com/KronicDeth/intellij-elixir/blob/042b07cf3615a4471252460c09123f86f13008e2/src/org/elixir_lang/credo/inspection_tool/Global.kt#L172
  - https://github.com/KronicDeth/intellij-elixir/blob/042b07cf3615a4471252460c09123f86f13008e2/src/org/elixir_lang/credo/inspection_tool/Global.kt#L188

### Read actions in UI-hot getters / navigation
- `PsiNamedElementImpl.getName` does `runReadAction` from getters used by Project View / navigation (can block EDT when a writer is pending):
  - https://github.com/KronicDeth/intellij-elixir/blob/042b07cf3615a4471252460c09123f86f13008e2/src/org/elixir_lang/psi/impl/PsiNamedElementImpl.kt#L23
  - https://github.com/KronicDeth/intellij-elixir/blob/042b07cf3615a4471252460c09123f86f13008e2/src/org/elixir_lang/psi/impl/PsiNamedElementImpl.kt#L44

- `CallImpl` uses `computeReadAction` / `runReadAction` for trivial PSI access in helper methods (called in presentation/navigation paths):
  - https://github.com/KronicDeth/intellij-elixir/blob/042b07cf3615a4471252460c09123f86f13008e2/src/org/elixir_lang/psi/impl/call/CallImpl.kt#L355
  - https://github.com/KronicDeth/intellij-elixir/blob/042b07cf3615a4471252460c09123f86f13008e2/src/org/elixir_lang/psi/impl/call/CallImpl.kt#L540
  - https://github.com/KronicDeth/intellij-elixir/blob/042b07cf3615a4471252460c09123f86f13008e2/src/org/elixir_lang/psi/impl/call/CallImpl.kt#L724
  - https://github.com/KronicDeth/intellij-elixir/blob/042b07cf3615a4471252460c09123f86f13008e2/src/org/elixir_lang/psi/impl/call/CallImpl.kt#L749

- `computeReadAction` is a plain `Application.runReadAction` wrapper:
  - https://github.com/KronicDeth/intellij-elixir/blob/042b07cf3615a4471252460c09123f86f13008e2/src/org/elixir_lang/mix/project/OtpApp.kt#L74

## Suggestions (needs verification)
- Add `ProgressManager.checkCanceled()` in the loops above and honor `Processor.process` return to stop early.
- For long-running PSI work, prefer cancellable `readAction { }`/`ReadAction.nonBlocking` rather than blocking `runReadAction` (2025.3 Fast UI).
- Avoid starting read actions in UI-hot getters; rely on callers already under read lock or use cached/stub values.

## Evidence / artifacts
[threadDump-20260213-084719-1770972439681.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/25287285/threadDump-20260213-084719-1770972439681.txt)
[IDEA_thread_dump_analysis.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/25287324/IDEA_thread_dump_analysis.txt)

title: Usage of internal APIs blocking release 23.0.0
author: joshuataylor
body:
Hey @sh41 - just got this email back from JetBrains, using Internal APIs isn't allowed (:().

> The [latest update of your plugin](https://plugins.jetbrains.com/plugin/7522-elixir/edit/versions/stable/952726) uses an Internal API, which is private and cannot be used outside the IntelliJ Platform itself.
> Please proceed with the [next steps described in this article](https://plugins.jetbrains.com/docs/intellij/api-internal.html) to resolve the issue.

It's [/src/org/elixir_lang/mix/project/_import/step/ElixirSdkForModuleStep.kt](https://github.com/KronicDeth/intellij-elixir/blob/7feff75866f4e7244fd53b57d809c2887cf417f7/src/org/elixir_lang/mix/project/_import/step/ElixirSdkForModuleStep.kt) I believe.

I'll have a look tomorrow to port this to what the recommended code is. :+1:

title: Trouble setting up env for contribution - build fails - getQuoterDeps fails - elixir installed with asdf - No version is set for command mix
author: KarimElsayad247
body: I cloned master branch and followed the steps in `CONTRIBUTION.MD` (which BTW seemed a little outdated, at least when it comes to importing the project) Build fails with this error ``` > Task :getQuoterDeps FAILED No version is set for command mix Consider adding one of the following versions in your config file at /home/karim/projects/intellij-elixir/cache/elixir-1.13.4-intellij_elixir-2.1.0/.tool-versions elixir el118 elixir 1.18 elixir 1.13.4 Execution failed for task ':getQuoterDeps'. > Process 'command 'mix'' finished with non-zero exit value 126 ``` The directory `cache/elixir-1.13.4-intellij_elixir-2.1.0` doesn't contain a .tool-versions to begin with. ## Software and OS Details ### Gradle and java ### IntelliJ ``` IntelliJ IDEA 2025.3.2 Build #IU-253.30387.90, built on January 22, 2026 Source revision: 61f1e2a0a8f30 Runtime version: 21.0.9+10-b1163.94 amd64 (JCEF 137.0.17) VM: OpenJDK 64-Bit Server VM by JetBrains s.r.o. title: Heex-file support seems not to be included in the 22.0.1 release
author: michael-lemonbeat
body: **Describe the bug** Thank you very much for your tireless work on this plugin. I really appreciate your work and I can well imagine that you receive a lot of requests. Unfortunately, I have a question regarding release 22.0.1. It is suggested that this release includes support for Heex files (thanks to the amazing work from mwnciau (Simon James) (PR #3696), we now have HEEx support!), but I still see the open PR here https://github.com/KronicDeth/intellij-elixir/pull/3696 And somehow, my setup (IntelliJ 2025.3 + plugin 22.0.1) does not recognize any Heex files as such. I assume that support will be added in the near future. Is my assumption correct? **To Reproduce** Steps to reproduce the behavior: No file type "Heex" in the "file types" settings. All heex-files are shown as regular text files. **Expected behavior** Support of the heex-files. **Screenshots** **Logs** **Desktop:** - OS: MacOS Tahoe - Version 26.2 **Erlang:** - Installer asdf - Version 27 **Elixir:** - Installer asdf - Version 19.1 **Plugin:** - Version 22.0.1 **Additional context**

title: HEEx or ~H sigil does not recognize functional components .component
author: kennedyoliveira
body: **Describe the bug** Version [v22.0.0](https://github.com/KronicDeth/intellij-elixir/releases/tag/v22.0.0) added support for HEEx and the ~H sigil, but it's not working as described in Windows 11 WebStorm, the HTML is working but no Elixir code completion or identification of functional components , they show as white and are not clickable, even when defined in the same file. Noticed heex files does not work automatically, had to manually associate them to get the html part to work, but elixir syntax is not working, neither on heex files nor on regular .ex files with `~H` sigil. **To Reproduce** Steps to reproduce the behavior: 1. Create a new phoenix project 2. Go to any of the default `heex` files or the `core_components` module 3. Check any of the functional components like `Layouts.theme_toggle` **Expected behavior** Syntax highlight/auto completion for elixir code and automatically assigning of heex files. **Screenshots** **Logs** If reporting a hang or other performance issue, please attach the [thread dumps](https://intellij-support.jetbrains.com/hc/en-us/articles/206544899-Getting-a-thread-dump-when-IDE-hangs-and-doesn-t-respond). **Desktop:** - OS: Windows 11 - Version 25H2 Build 26200.7462 **Erlang:** - Installer [Downloaded installer from website] - Version [28] **Elixir:** - Installer [Downloaded installer from the website] - Version [1.19.4] **Plugin:** - Version [22.0.1] **Additional context** Setup according to the docs, installed erlang plugin, elixir plugin, added erlang sdk, then elixir sdk, it's showing "Elixir Unknown" in the widget but it also says `Configured Correctly`, other things work, like code completion for regular files, mix actions, tests etc.

title: MIX_HOME can point to the wrong directory when using ASDF/MISE type managers
author: sh41
body:
**Describe the bug**
The `MIX_HOME` environment variable is not changed when the SDK is changed. It seems to be whatever it was set to when the IDE was first opened. 

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. On a system with `mise` installed, create two projects with different versions of Elixir (i.e. `mise use elixir@1.17` & `mise use elixir@1.19`)
2. On the command line, change to the directory for one of the projects and run `idea`. 
3. Use the file menu to open the other project
4. Create a script named `print_mix_home.exs` with the following content: 
```
IO.puts("MIX_HOME: " <> (System.get_env("MIX_HOME") || "not set"))
IO.puts("Elixir version: " <> System.version())
```
5. Create a run configuration, type: "Elixir" with `elixir arguments`: "print_mix_home.exs".
6. Run the run configuration and look at the output in the run console. 


**Expected behavior**
```
MIX_HOME: /home/user/.local/share/mise/installs/elixir/1.17.3-otp-27/.mix
Elixir version: 1.17.3
```
**Actual behaviour:**
```
MIX_HOME: /home/user/.local/share/mise/installs/elixir/1.19.4-otp-28/.mix
Elixir version: 1.17.3
```

**Desktop:**
 - OS: Ubuntu
 - Version 24.04

**Plugin:**
- Version 22.0.0 running on 2025.3.1


I'm happy to attempt a fix for this as part of the WSL work I'm currently looking into. 

The real world issue with this is that when you attempt to run mix, it will complain that hex isn't installed, or that it is compiled to the wrong BEAM version. The reason that happens is because it's looking in the wrong place for the files. 

This is the output when attempting to run tests with a 1.19 MIX_HOME and 1.17 executables: 
```
17:13:51.651 [error] beam/beam_load.c(150): Error loading module 'Elixir.Hex':
  corrupt atom table



17:13:51.690 [error] beam/beam_load.c(150): Error loading module 'Elixir.Hex':
  corrupt atom table


Mix requires the Hex package manager to fetch dependencies
Shall I install Hex? (if running non-interactively, use "mix local.hex --force") [Yn] 
```

You can't actually install hex because the console is read only in tests, and if you try to do it from the console it just installs in the correct location and doesn't fix the issue with the tests.

title: Can't open directory containing Elixir project (2025.3 issue?)
author: joshuataylor

Opening a non-elixir project works.

Not sure what is going on, it seems to be from the merge.

Looking into it.

Using `runIde`:

```sh
  at kotlinx.coroutines.BuildersKt__Builders_commonKt.withContext(Builders.common.kt:166)

  at kotlinx.coroutines.scheduling.CoroutineScheduler$Worker.runDefaultDispatcherTask(CoroutineScheduler.kt:1194)

```

title: Elixir SDK Not Being Applied After Selection in IntelliJ IDEA
author: zubair-ali-iz
body: I am experiencing an issue in IntelliJ IDEA where the Elixir SDK is not being applied to my project. When I click the "Setup Elixir SDK" message at the top of the IDE, a popup appears with a dropdown list of available SDK versions. However, after selecting the desired version (e.g., 1.18.2-otp-26), the SDK does not get applied, and the popup remains unchanged. This prevents me from using Elixir in my project. I’ve already checked the permissions and verified that the SDK is installed correctly via asdf, but the issue persists in IntelliJ IDEA. No clear error message appears, making it difficult to identify the root cause.
